summary: Backend-only testing for SkillBridge. Created pytest suite covering /api/health, /api/analyze-text (JSON), /api/analyze (PDF multipart + 400 validation), /api/history (list/detail/delete), /api/chat + /api/chat/{session_id}, and cross-session isolation. All 10 tests passed against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Endee + Gemini + sentence-transformers (MiniLM-L6-v2) integrations are healthy end-to-end. No mocks used; real network calls.

minor.issue: Catches all exceptions and returns 500 even when the upstream cause is a validation error (e.g., empty jd_text). Consider mirroring /analyze and raising 400 explicitly when resume_text or jd_text are blank.
Uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown'); migrate to FastAPI lifespan handler.
No pagination beyond `limit` query param (no skip/offset). Fine for MVP but document for scale.

action_items: Optional: add explicit 400 validation in /api/analyze-text for empty resume_text / jd_text (currently surfaces as 500).
Optional: replace @app.on_event('shutdown') with FastAPI lifespan to silence deprecation warnings.
First analyze call is ~30-45s due to MiniLM model download; consider preloading the model on FastAPI startup (e.g., call get_embedder() once at app start) so the first user request is fast.
critical_code_review_comments: rag_pipeline.analyze() runs blocking CPU work (sentence-transformers encode + Endee upsert) inside an async route — under concurrent load this will block the event loop. Recommend offloading _ingest/_retrieve via run_in_executor or making them async.
vector_store.EndeeStore.upsert/query are sync calls inside async handlers — same event-loop concern as above.

server.py line 191 history list query uses {'_id': 0} (good), but history_detail returns the entire stored doc including the large `result` dict and full resume_text/jd_text — fine functionally; just confirm intentional payload size.
Title parser _title_from_text returns first short line; if resumes start with 'CURRICULUM VITAE' it can become noisy — minor UX nit.
Singletons (_store, embedder) are not lazily-thread-safe; first concurrent burst could double-init. Use a lock or initialize at startup.
seed_data_creation: Two analyze-text records created (Senior Backend + Frontend React) plus one record created and then deleted by test_history_delete. Throwaway chat messages persisted under their session_ids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_skillbridge_backend.py -v . Public BASE_URL is REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. First run is ~90s end-to-end (MiniLM download + Gemini calls). The suite uses session-scoped fixtures so analyze-text is invoked twice (sessions A & B) and one PDF upload — total ~5-6 LLM calls. /api/health must return all true booleans. Mongo collections used: analyses, chats. Endee index name: skillbridge.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all happy paths green.
